instructions: Add the flour, dry yeast, salt and sugar to the bowl and mix.

We start the dough and see if we need to add water during. We do not add much water from the beginning so that we do not have to add much more flour to the dough.
We knead about 10′′. The dough will come out mode, neither too soft nor too hard.

paleo-applesauce-pancakes.name: Paleo Applesauce Pancakes
instructions: Whisk eggs lightly in a large bowl. Add applesauce and vanilla extract; mix well to combine.
Combine almond flour, tapioca flour, cinnamon, baking soda, and salt in a separate bowl.
Add to egg mixture and mix until just combined. Add water until pancake batter is desired thickness. Set batter aside to rest for 15 minutes.
Melt coconut oil on a griddle over medium-high heat. Drop batter by large spoonfuls onto the griddle and cook until bubbles form in the center and the edges are dry, 3 to 4 minutes.
Flip and cook until golden on the other side, 2 to 3 minutes. Repeat with remaining batter.
Serve and enjoy!

pan-fried-jerk-pork-chops.name: Pan-fried Jerk Pork Chops
instructions: Combine jerk seasoning, salt, and black pepper in a small bowl; set rub aside.
Heat oil in a large skillet over medium heat; add about 1 tablespoon jerk rub. Add onion; cook and stir until soft, about 3 minutes.

Meanwhile, sprinkle 1/2 remaining jerk rub onto one side of each pork chop. Poke 4 or 5 holes into each chop, using the tip of a knife; drizzle 1/2 lime juice over top. Place chops, seasoned-sides down, into the hot skillet.

Cook until brown, 5 to 7 minutes. While chops cook, sprinkle remaining jerk rub onto top sides of chops; poke 4 or 5 holes into each chop using the tip of a knife. Drizzle remaining lime juice over top. Flip chops; cook until brown and no longer pink in centers, 5 to 7 minutes more. An instant-read thermometer inserted into centers should read 63°C (63 degrees C).

pancetta-crisps-with-goat-cheese-and-fig.name: Pancetta Crisps With Goat Cheese And Figs

ingredients.item: Fresh figs or 6 plump dried mission figs
ingredients.prep: halved, or 12 small fresh persimmon wedges

instructions: Preheat oven to 204°C. Place pancetta in single layer on large rimmed baking sheet. Bake until crisp, 12 to 15 minutes. Cool 15 minutes; transfer to platter. Top each slice with 1 arugula leaf, 1 teaspoon goat cheese, 1/4 teaspoon jam, and 1 fig half or persimmon wedge; sprinkle with thyme and freshly ground pepper.

panko-crusted-chicken-with-mustard-maple.name: Panko-crusted Chicken With Mustard-maple Pan Sauce
instructions: Using meat mallet or rolling pin, pound chicken in resealable plastic bag to 1/3- to 1/2-inch thickness. Whisk egg, parsley, and 2 teaspoons Dijon mustard in large bowl. Place chicken in egg mixture; turn to coat. Sprinkle chicken with salt and pepper. Dip each chicken piece in panko; turn to coat.
Heat oil in large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. Add chicken; cook until brown and cooked through, about 4 minutes per side.
Meanwhile, whisk broth, syrup, coarse-grained mustard, and remaining 2 tablespoons Dijon mustard in glass measuring cup.
Transfer chicken to plates. Add broth mixture to skillet; boil until reduced to 3/4 cup, stirring occasionally, about 4 minutes. Add butter; whisk until melted. Spoon sauce alongside chicken.

parisian-flan-teleculinaria.name: Parisian Flan
instructions: Start the oven at 190°C. Light a pot with the milk and vanilla essence and let it boil.
On the other hand, mix the eggs with sugar, butter and moreena flour until they are homogeneous. Add warm milk, in yarn, mixing well. Take it back to the fire, always stirring until it thickens.
Unroll the broken dough and dispose in a 22 cm diameter tart previously lined with vegetable paper. Pick the bottom with a fork and stuff it with the cream.
Wrap the edge of the dough inside and put it in the oven for about 30 minutes, or until it is well golden. Take it off, let it cool down and unshape it.

peach-and-prosciutto-sandwich.name: Peach And Prosciutto Sandwich
instructions: Gently toss peaches, vinegar, 1 Tbsp. chili crisp, and a pinch of salt in a small bowl to combine.
Place baguette, cut side up, on a plate. Layer cheese over bottom half and top with peach mixture, then basil leaves, prosciutto, and more chili crisp. Close sandwich; cut in half.

peachy-white-sangria.name: Peachy White Sangria
instructions: Stir Moscato wine, lemon-lime soda, brandy, peach schnapps, peaches, strawberries, mangos, pineapple chunks, and raspberries together in a large container.
Chill the sangria for 8 hours or overnight. Serve and enjoy!

peanut-ginger-double-deckers.name: Peanut-ginger Double-deckers
instructions: Preheat oven to 177°C (175 degrees C). Line a baking sheet with a silicone baking mat or parchment paper.

Peanut Butter Dough: Put butter, white sugar, and brown sugar in a bowl. Cream together with a mixer until butter clings to sides of bowl and is uniform in color, about 3 minutes. Mix in peanut butter, baking powder, baking soda, and salt until incorporated. Scrape down sides of bowl, then mix in egg yolk until incorporated. Add flour; mix on low speed until a dough forms. Cover with plastic wrap.

Ginger-Molasses Dough: Put white sugar, butter, ginger, baking soda, cinnamon, cloves, and salt in a bowl. Cream together with a mixer until butter clings to sides of bowl and is uniform in color. Scrape down sides of bowl; mix in egg until incorporated. Mix in molasses until incorporated; mix in flour until incorporated. Mix in crystallized ginger until incorporated. Scraping bowl between additions.

Roll a walnut-size ball (1 1/2 tablespoons) of ginger-molasses dough in turbinado sugar. Set on prepared baking sheet; flatten slightly with your palm. Brush top with water. Roll a marble-size ball (2 teaspoons) of peanut butter dough, set atop disk, then use a fork to flatten dough and make a crisscross pattern. Repeat with remaining dough. Bake until edges of top cookies start to turn golden brown, 10 to 12 minutes. Cool on sheet 10 minutes. Transfer to a wire rack to cool completely.

pear-upside-down-graham-cake.name: Pear Upside-down Graham Cake
instructions: Preheat the oven to 177°C (175 degrees C). Grease a 9-inch pan with 2-inch sides and line the bottom with a round of parchment paper.
Add butter pieces to the pan. Place in the preheating oven until melted. Sprinkle brown sugar on top. Arrange sliced pears in a circular pattern over the butter and brown sugar. Set aside.
Whisk graham flour, all-purpose flour, baking powder, baking soda, ginger, salt, and cinnamon together in a medium bowl.

Combine butter, brown sugar, oil, and honey in a stand mixer fitted with the paddle attachment. Beat on medium speed for 2 minutes, scraping the sides of the bowl as necessary. Add eggs and vanilla extract; beat for 1 minute. Add flour mixture in 3 additions, alternating with milk; beat briefly after each addition.
Pour batter carefully over the pears. Smooth the top.

Bake in the preheated oven until golden brown and a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean, about 25 minutes. Cool cake for 10 minutes. Invert onto a wire rack and peel off parchment paper. Allow to cool completely.

pistachio-crusted-rack-of-lamb.name: Pistachio-crusted Rack Of Lamb

ingredients.item: Salt and ground black pepper to taste

instructions: Preheat the oven to 204°C (200 degrees C). Line a baking sheet with aluminum foil.
Generously season each rack of lamb with herbes de Provence, salt, and pepper.
Heat vegetable oil in a large skillet over high heat. Place lamb into the skillet and cook until browned on all sides, 6 to 8 minutes. Transfer to the prepared baking sheet.

Stir pistachios, bread crumbs, melted butter, and olive oil together in a small bowl; season with salt and pepper. Spread mustard over the fatty side of lamb, then press pistachio mixture into mustard.

Bake in the preheated oven until crust is golden and lamb is pink in the center, 20 to 25 minutes. An instant-read thermometer inserted into the center should read at least 54°C (54 degrees C) for medium doneness.
Transfer lamb to a plate and let rest for 10 minutes before slicing.

poached-eggs-over-scafata.name: Poached Eggs Over Scafata

ingredients.prep: cut on a diagonal into 1” pieces

ingredients.item: Fresh fava beans (from about 1 lb. pods) or frozen fava beans
Shelled fresh peas (from about  0.5 lb. pods) or frozen peas

instructions: Heat onion, chile, garlic, asparagus, and ¼ cup oil in a medium saucepan over medium heat until oil begins to bubble. Reduce heat to medium-low, cover, and cook, shaking pan occasionally, until asparagus is crisp-tender, about 2 minutes. Add scallions, fava beans, and peas; cover and cook until fava beans are just tender, about 2 minutes.
Add escarole, basil, and mint and cook, tossing, until escarole is wilted and asparagus is very tender, about 2 minutes; season with salt.

Meanwhile, bring 2” water to a boil in a large saucepan; reduce heat so water is at a gentle simmer and add vinegar. Crack an egg into a small bowl, then gently slide egg into water. Repeat with remaining eggs, waiting until whites of eggs in water are opaque before adding the next egg (about 30 seconds apart). Poach until whites are set but yolks are still runny, about 3 minutes. Using a slotted spoon, transfer eggs to paper towels as they are done.
Serve scafata topped with eggs and Pecorino and drizzled with oil.
DO AHEAD: Eggs can be poached 2 hours ahead; place in a bowl of ice water and chill. Reheat in barely simmering water 1 minute just before serving.

pomegranate-liqueur-argiro.name: Pomegranate Liqueur
instructions: Clean the pomegranates first and place the seeds in a glass container with airtight closure.
Add the cognac, tsipouro, sugar and cinnamon wood.
Close the container well and leave it in a shaded and cool place for 20-25 days, shaking regularly to dissolve the sugar.
Then, strain the liqueur with double tulpan and transfer to clean, glass bottles.
Pomegranate liqueur is traditionally served in small glasses of liquor, plain or slightly cool.
For a more impressive presentation, we can serve it with ice cubes, pomegranate seeds and lime slices, creating a special and aromatic drink.
It fits beautifully as an aperitif or digestif after eating. It can also be used as a base for cocktails with tonic, prosecco or soda..

instructions: Pound pork steaks between 2 layers of plastic wrap to ¼" thick. Place flour in a shallow bowl. Lightly beat eggs in another shallow bowl. Toss breadcrumbs and cornstarch in a third shallow bowl; season with kosher salt and pepper.

Working one at a time, season cutlets with kosher salt and pepper, then dredge in flour, shaking off excess. Transfer to bowl with egg and turn to coat. Lift from bowl, allowing excess to drip off. Coat with breadcrumb mixture, pressing to adhere.

Heat vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high. Working in 2 batches, cook cutlets, shaking pan occasionally, until golden brown and cooked through, about 3 minutes per side. Transfer to a wire rack; season with sea salt.

Combine fennel, fennel fronds, asparagus, onion, and parsley in a medium bowl. Toss with lemon juice and olive oil; season with kosher salt and pepper. Serve salad alongside cutlets with lemon wedges for squeezing over.

instructions: Place your chosen glasses or bowls in the freezer for half an hour. Soften the gelatine sheets in a bowl of cold water for 3–5 minutes.
Place 2 tablespoons of water and the sugar in a saucepan over a medium-low heat. Stir until the sugar dissolves, then remove from the heat.

Remove the gelatine sheets from their soaking water and squeeze out any excess liquid. Transfer the softened gelatine to the hot syrup and stir until dissolved. Set aside to cool for 5 minutes, then pour the prosecco into the syrup. Remove the glasses or bowls from the freezer, pour in the jelly mixture and return to the freezer for half an hour.
Take the glasses out of the freezer and place 8-10 raspberries in each jelly, pressing down slightly. Chill the jellies in the fridge for 2-3 hours until fully set.

pudding-pops.name: Pudding Pops
instructions: Beat milk, evaporated milk, instant pudding, and sugar in a bowl until thoroughly combined.
Pour into ice pop molds and freeze until firm, about 3 hours.

puff-pastry-mummy-sausages-for-halloween.name: Puff Pastry Mummy Sausages For Halloween (terrifyingly Recipe)
puff-pastry-mummy-sausages-for-halloween.original_name: Salchichas Momia de hojaldre para Halloween (receta terroríficamente fácil)
instructions: Let's pull the paddle dough With a knife we cut tipple strips and put them into the Frankfurt sausages First we cover the body of the mummy and leave an uncovered part where their eyes will go. We finally cover the top of the mummy's head with another tipple strap.

We purify the oven at 180 ° C We put the mummy sausages onto an oven plate We pinch the pail dough with a beamed egg We place small sugar eyes (you can buy them here). You can put two chocolate droplets as well.
We bake at 180 ° C with heat up and down for 15 minutes or till the paddle's golden We get out and serve hot

pumpkin-bread-in-the-oven-or-in-the-air-.name: Pumpkin Bread In The Oven Or In The Air Fryer
pumpkin-bread-in-the-oven-or-in-the-air-.original_name: Pan de calabaza casero al horno o en freidora de aire

ingredients.item: 500 g de harina de fuerza (unas 4 tazas)
25 g de levadura fresca o 7 g de seca
50 ml olive oil (¼ cup) or melted butter

instructions: We started with pumpkin purée. We can cook or roast 300 g pumpkin, scrape well and shredded up to a thick purée. It's important that you have no excess water so that the mass doesn't get too wet.
In a big bowl we mix 100 ml of tibia milk with 25 g of fresh yeast and a tablespoon of sugar. We let them rest about 10 minutes till I moist.
We added pumpkin purée, 50 g brown sugar, 50 ml olive oil and half a teaspoon of salt. We mix till everything's integrated.
We put up with 500 g of flour, and we put them into a soft and somewhat sticky mass. If necessary, we add one or two more spoons of flour.

We amassed for about 10 minutes on an enlarged surface to gain an elastic mass. We form a ball, put it into a slightly grown bowl and cover with a cloth. We let them get up for 1 and a half hour or till they double their size.
Una vez levada, la desgasificamos suavemente y le damos forma de hogaza o la colocamos en un molde alargado. Tapamos y dejamos reposar otros 30 minutos.
We purify the oven at 180 ° C with heat up and down. We bake for about 30-35 minutes till as we hit the base they sound empty.
Dejamos enfriar sobre una rejilla antes de cortar.

pumpkin-conchas.name: Pumpkin Conchas
instructions: Combine flour, pumpkin puree, warm milk, egg yolk, melted butter, sugar, active dry yeast, salt, and vanilla extract in the bowl of a stand mixer with the dough hook attachment. Mix until a smooth, slightly elastic ball forms or knead by hand for about 5 minutes.
Place the dough in a lightly greased mixing bowl, cover, and let rise in a warm place until doubled in size, about 2 hours.

While dough is rising, combine powdered sugar, flour, butter, cinnamon, allspice, nutmeg, salt, and vanilla extract in a bowl for the topping. Mix with your hands or a spatula until a moldable dough forms. Mix in orange food coloring if you like.

Divide topping dough into 6 equal portions and shape them into balls; set onto a parchment lined baking sheet. Cover each ball with a sheet of plastic and press down with something flat and heavy (like the bottom of a glass or measuring cup) into a flat circle. Take the tip of a knife and score a pumpkin design into the topping. Place in the refrigerator to allow butter to firm up.

After the dough rises, transfer it onto a work surface and press down to remove the air. Cut into 6 equal portions and roll each portion into a smooth ball. Transfer balls onto a Silpat-lined baking sheet and press down to flatten lightly. Place the topping on top of the dough balls. Allow to proof until doubled in size, about 45 minutes.
Preheat the oven to 191°C (190 degrees C).
Bake in the center of a preheated oven until puffed up and golden, about 20 minutes. Allow to cool to room temperature, about 20 minutes.

pumpkin-fritters-recipe-comedera.name: Pumpkin Fritters Recipe
instructions: Prepare pumpkin: cut up 200 g pumpkin, bake at 180 ″ 3 ″ for 25 minutes till soft and make a fine purée.
Mix the dry ingredients: in a bowl, mix the flour, baking powder, cinnamon, ginger, brown sugar and salt.
It incorporates the egg and milk into pumpkin purée. Mix well to integrate.
Add dry ingredients to pumpkin purée and mix to achieve a homogenous, slightly sticky mass.
Warm oil in a frying pan with medium-high heat. To test the temperature, place a small dough ball: if it bubbles gently, the oil's ready.
With a spoon, it forms small portions of dough and frying them till they are golden and crispy out, about 3-4 minutes by side.
Remove and place onto absorbing paper. Sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon to suit.
It serves hot, with honey or syrup if you wish an extra touch of sweetness.

pumpkin-spaghetti-with-avocado-pesto-sau.name: Pumpkin Spaghetti With Avocado Pesto Sauce
pumpkin-spaghetti-with-avocado-pesto-sau.original_name: Μακαρόνια κολοκύθας με σάλτσα πέστο αβοκάντο

ingredients.item: 4 tablespoons of pine nuts or other dried fruit
4 tablespoons of pine nuts or other dried fruit

instructions: We cut the pumpkin along first, like we cut a melon. Carefully remove the seeds and fibers from the inside.
Brush the pumpkin's flesh with olive oil and add it lightly.
Place the two halves of the pumpkin with the cut side downwards on a baking pan lined with parchment paper.
Bake in a preheated oven, at 200°C in the air, on the medium rack, for about 40 minutes, until it softens well.
The fly will acquire a deep, vivid yellow color and be soft enough to be “drawed” into fibers — forming the spaghetti pumpkin characteristics.
Let the pumpkin cool for 5-10 minutes until we can catch it without burning.
We flip the two halves so we can have a visual on the flesh. With two forks, gently scratch the inside, pulling the flesh into fibers.
Divide the “sangetti” pumpkin into 4 bowls or another them in a large bowl.
Pour with avocado pesto and, if you want, sprinkle with some parmesan for extra flavor.

pumpkin-spice-blossoms.name: Pumpkin Spice Blossoms

ingredients.prep: such as Hershey's® Pumpkin Spice Kisses

instructions: Stir flour, cocoa, pumpkin pie spice, baking soda, and salt together in a bowl.
Combine butter, white sugar, and brown sugar in a large bowl. Beat with an electric mixer until light and fluffy. Add eggs and vanilla extract; beat well. Mix in flour mixture gradually.
Chill cookie dough for 1 hour. Meanwhile, unwrap the candy kisses.
Preheat the oven to 191°C (190 degrees C). Line cookie sheets with parchment paper.
Roll dough into 1 1/2-inch balls. Flatten just enough to keep from rolling off the cookie sheets.

Bake in the preheated oven until edges are set and starting to brown, 8 to 10 minutes. Slide parchment paper onto wire cooling racks. Firmly push a candy kiss into the center of each cookie. Let cool completely.
